summaries.79982: Comments clarify this is working-as-intended because TextEditingController is a ValueNotifier<TextEditingValue> and fires on any value change including selection; the separate focus-change issue was split into #86752 which has been addressed. A workaround using TextFormField's onChange callback is noted for text-only changes, and a DartPad showing a text-vs-selection check is linked.
summaries.165951: Root cause identified in comments: selection only updates on drag events, so mouse-wheel scrolling without pointer movement produces no drag-update events, leaving the selection unchanged. The same behavior is reproducible with SelectableText. No fix has been submitted; the commenter invited PRs but is not actively working on it.
summaries.93500: Root cause traced to the cursor width adding an extra pixel of space between hint text and suffix text. A comment proposed negative cursorWidth as a workaround but noted an assert blocks it in debug mode. A Unicode narrow no-break space ('\u202f') is offered as a practical workaround. PR #93634 was started by @Reekoooo but not completed; the team called for someone to apply the review comments from that PR and open a new one.
summaries.153811: Confirmed on stable 3.24.1 and master 3.25.0 on both macOS and web. Changing the macOS input source to German makes Alt/Option Right equivalent to AltGr, yet Flutter reports only Alt Right instead of AltGr. No fix or PR mentioned; CC to @dkwingsmt.
summaries.78864: Comments are limited to a compile error in the original sample and a single confirmation screenshot; no root cause, fix, or additional platform data added beyond what is in the body.
summaries.130652: Root cause is a focus conflict: Flutter's TextField retains focus and caret when the WebView input is tapped, resulting in two simultaneous carets on screen. The physical keyboard issue is confirmed to be independent of whether the virtual keyboard is disabled. No fix proposed; issue kept open for team attention.
summaries.154816: Confirmed device-specific to OPPO Find X5 Pro and later reported on Honor Magic 6 Pro, Samsung S24 Ultra, and OnePlus; the SwiftKey keyboard (Microsoft SwiftKey) is the common factor across all affected devices. The issue does not reproduce on Pixel or Samsung without SwiftKey. No fix or PR mentioned.
summaries.61069: Comments are largely me-too and bumps spanning multiple years with no root-cause analysis. One substantive comment links a related DevTools bug and notes @LongCatIsLooong is working on overflow ellipsis. The auto_size_text_field package is offered as an imperfect workaround; a TextPainter-based manual ellipsis workaround is also shared. No PR opened.
summaries.128209: Reproduced on Android (Realme 6, Android 11) on both stable and master; not confirmed on iOS because split-screen mode is unavailable on tested iOS devices. Reproducible with a Flutter app split alongside any other app (native or Flutter). Questions raised about whether Flutter does anything engine-side to support split-screen and whether screen insets are involved; no fix proposed.
summaries.120873: Root cause identified as the iOS keyboard notifications treating the floating shortcuts bar as "docked" on iOS 16 but as "floating" on iOS 15, causing the Flutter view to resize for the bar's height. @vashworth confirmed this is not intended and needs a fix. A comment references the relevant engine code in FlutterViewController.mm. Team iOS later flagged that fixing this requires an engine-side change to provide a different viewInset for the popup.
summaries.183392: Comments confirm the issue reproduces on Android with resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false and that even setting it to true (the default) does not fully scroll the input into view. iOS handles the scroll natively via WKWebView and works correctly. The fyi-text-input label was removed because this is considered a WebView input tag issue, not a Flutter text input issue.
summaries.150068: Discussion centers on the broader IME/platform API exposure strategy rather than iOS 18 formatting menu specifics. A separate tracking issue #150460 was created to concentrate efforts on generating bindings for the IME. The community (notably @matthew-carroll) called for transparency on the Flutter team's IME plans; @justinmc committed to investigating and creating an issue.
summaries.112198: Comments clarify the distinction between this narrow proposal (expose iOS's enablesReturnKeyAutomatically behavior) and the broader #93915 (dynamically enable/disable the action button at any time). The issue was closed as duplicate, then reopened. One comment suggests that because the keyboard is a separate window outside the widget tree, a closure API may be necessary for any dynamic enable/disable approach.
summaries.144759: Root cause identified: Samsung keyboard ignores the left-arrow key when the cursor is already at the visual left end of RTL text, because Flutter uses logical order for arrow key navigation while Samsung keyboard uses visual order. Gboard is not affected. On native, arrow key navigation moves in visual order which Flutter does not currently support; a commenter notes this is a long-standing architectural gap.
summaries.184557: Root cause identified in comments: both Flutter and WebKit dispatch keyboard events asynchronously; when Flutter re-dispatches an unhandled Enter key, WebKit's guard (`m_keyDownEventBeingResent`) is already null, so NSWindow resends the event to the first responder (WebKit) as if it were new, causing infinite repetition. The proposed fix is to rewrite macOS event handling to be synchronous, which is now feasible since the UI and platform threads are merged.
summaries.168322: Confirmed reproducible on iPhone XS running stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0. The issue is specific to iOS mobile browser (web platform). A similar visual glitch when scrolling is also reported by a commenter. The issue was added to the tracking list at #120613.
summaries.114952: Comments clarify the underlying design: InputDecoration.constraints constrains the container but not the error text area, and setting maxHeight too small clips the error message. Setting helperText to "" reserves space for error/helper/counter. The height difference between web and mobile is explained as VisualDensity defaulting to compact on desktop/web; ThemeData.visualDensity can override this. No PR opened.
summaries.156272: Confirmed reproducible on iOS 17.5 simulator as well, not specific to iOS 18. Suspected to be a platform view gesture handling issue rather than a plugin-level issue. Issue routed to team-ios; no fix or PR mentioned.
summaries.158180: Confirmed on iPad Air 5 with iPadOS 17.5.1 and on Flutter beta 3.27 and stable 3.22.3. Using SystemContextMenu (now the default) fixes the Apple Pencil tapping issue, so priority was downgraded to P3. A separate issue #159259 was filed for an assertion error that appears when using SystemContextMenu with Scribble.
summaries.159384: Root cause narrowed to Microsoft SwiftKey keyboard as the input method: usbHidUsage codes are correct with the default Android input method but wrong when SwiftKey is set as default, reproducible on both physical device and emulator. Confirmed on stable 3.24.5 and master 3.27.0. CC to @dkwingsmt.
summaries.180144: The team acknowledged the bug but indicated it is low priority because Android Desktop Mode changes the user-agent and other browser behaviors in ways Flutter's adaptive framework does not account for, and the recommended path is using Flutter's built-in adaptive features rather than relying on desktop-mode browser heuristics.
summaries.39466: A detailed analysis in comments confirms the bug persists: the cursor (cursorWidth: 2.0 by default) is drawn on top of hint text rather than under it. The Material spec does not define cursor width; the Figma kit and Compose both show the cursor under the hint text. The fix proposed is to paint hint text over the cursor (z-order change), matching Compose behavior.
summaries.184240: Comments are only triage routing: the issue was forwarded to the framework team and CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong. No root cause analysis or fix discussed.
summaries.141557: Production crash (IndexOutOfBoundsException: invalid composing start/selection start) reproduced across Android 8.1–14 on Flutter 3.16–3.32 but cannot be reliably reproduced locally. A comment links it to #100319 (typing while holding a selection endpoint). Multiple users confirm it persists through Flutter 3.32.1 with no fix; no PR or workaround provided.
summaries.168451: Root cause: the default groupId for EditableText is the same for every instance, so all TextFields share one TapRegion group and tapping a second field does not trigger onTapOutside on the first. A workaround of passing a unique groupId per TextField is noted, but a commenter reports it still did not work in their specific list-based setup. Confirmed on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0.
summaries.172689: Root cause identified in comments: PR #157399 introduced a `_justResumed` flag stored per EditableTextState that is set on app resume for every TextField but only reset when each field individually gains focus, causing the auto-select behavior to be unreliable after backgrounding. Confirmed on stable 3.32.7 and master 3.33.0 on both web and desktop.
summaries.46728: Comments add a clear explanation of the root cause: setting text during build fires listeners synchronously, which can call setState and invalidate the current build frame. @Hixie confirmed this is the reason and suggested the general "no side effects in build" guidance should be added to State.build and StatelessWidget.build API docs; the issue will be addressed as part of this ticket.
summaries.86471: Confirmed reproduced on master: when text with an active composing underline is present and the cursor is placed in the middle of it, typing adds characters at the end rather than at the cursor position. Safari/UITextField handles this correctly. The issue is specifically about inserting within an uncommitted composing region, not just at any position.
summaries.162732: Comments clarify this is a feature request for an inline suffix position option (suffixTextPosition). The suggested workaround of overriding buildTextSpan fails with a RangeError when suffix text is included in the selection. A TextInputFormatter approach was suggested but the reporter notes users expect the raw value without the suffix symbol.
summaries.145887: Root cause isolated: setting `_textController.text = v` inside the onChange callback (reassigning the controller reference rather than setting its text property) disrupts composing state on Android with Korean input, but not on iOS. After the Gboard update this is not reproducible via the original code; the issue only manifests with the misuse pattern.
summaries.104315: Confirmed on iPad Air 5 (iPadOS 17.5.1) with Flutter beta 3.27 and stable 3.22.3: Apple Pencil Scribble always capitalizes the first letter regardless of the case drawn. CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong; no fix or PR mentioned.
summaries.178609: Related issue #153907 linked. A commenter notes that third-party speech-to-text apps (Wispr Flow, Spokenly) that send synthetic key events are also broken by the same root cause. No fix proposed.
summaries.77553: Root cause identified: the intrinsic width calculation in InputDecorator does not include the width of the error text, pointing to the relevant code at input_decorator.dart around line 1186-1199. No fix or PR mentioned; the behavior is considered by design but potentially wrong.
summaries.181474: The issue is specific to iPadOS 26 with TextInputType.number, where the floating numeric keypad is dismissed but the TextField retains focus, preventing keyboard re-presentation. Additional comment notes that M-chip iPads also have trouble showing the number keypad at all on iPadOS 26. A workaround is provided: switching keyboardType to TextInputType.text with a numeric FilteringTextInputFormatter for iOS 26+.
summaries.181231: Root cause explained: nested SelectionArea regions each register a RawGestureDetector with the same recognizers; when a right-click is held long enough, the BaseTapGestureRecognizer exceeds its deadline and fires indiscriminately, triggering both the nested and outer menus. Wrapping with SelectionContainer.disabled also fails to prevent this. Confirmed on stable 3.38.7 and master 3.41.0.
summaries.99606: Comments confirm Android has a native API (EditorInfo.hintLocales, API 24+) but iOS has no public API. A detailed feasibility analysis concludes a plugin alone is insufficient because the language hint must be set at the engine level (TextInputPlugin on Android, FlutterTextInputPlugin on iOS). Windows has been solved via FFI by one commenter. Issue remains open with no PR.
summaries.107578: Comments flag that third-party app-specific context menu buttons (e.g., AnkiDroid) are tracked separately in #107603. The iOS Translate button can now likely be added using the Translation API announced at WWDC24. Android's ACTION_PROCESS_TEXT mechanism for third-party buttons is confirmed possible per #107603. Linked PRs: Look Up #130532, Search Web #131898, Share engine #44554.
summaries.107127: A commenter (@Renzo-Olivares) acknowledged the issues and committed to adding toJSON(), cleaning up fromJSON(), addressing silent failures via #107426, and clarifying composing/selection terminology. The public constant naming proposal was accepted by @justinmc. The issue was later unassigned from @Renzo-Olivares with no status update.
summaries.82708: Root cause partially identified: when CMD+F searches for text that matches content in a Flutter TextField, the field loses focus and cannot regain it by tapping. A separate macOS desktop commenter reports random loss of selection/paste/backspace after ~20 minutes (unrelated). PR #80899 was cited as possibly related but confirmed unrelated by @mdebbar.
summaries.169104: Comments were unable to reliably reproduce the reported auto-selection behavior on web outside of the reporter's specific macOS 15 + Chrome setup. Triage confirmed the issue could not be replicated on macOS or Windows by other contributors. The issue remains open for team tracking with no confirmed root cause or platform pattern.
summaries.97866: Comments confirm several of the missing behaviors still reproduced on Flutter 2.10.0, specifically: autoscroll while holding mouse outside bounds (requires movement, should not require it), autoscroll with keyboard Shift+Arrow beyond visible area, and Ctrl+Shift+Home/End not scrolling to offset. The issue is treated as a desktop proposal; CC'd to @justinmc.
summaries.40675: Comments discuss a proposal for AutovalidateMode enum (onChange, onBlur/unfocus) as a cleaner API. A commenter provides a workaround using a Focus wrapper around the Form with onFocusChange to trigger validation. The discussion notes that the current approach of using onUnfocus validation has a UX flaw: if the user edits an invalid field, the error message persists even as they type corrections.
summaries.179527: Root cause identified: non-const WidgetSpan instances are treated as new objects on rebuild, causing RenderParagraph's internal _SelectableFragments to be disposed and recreated, which resets the selection. Using const WidgetSpan avoids the issue. The same behavior affects SelectableText.rich with dynamic strings.
summaries.161076: Comments confirm the issue: adding a suffix icon shifts the text baseline downward relative to a TextField without a suffix, when both are constrained to the same height (e.g., 50px SizedBox). A commenter (@justinmc) asks whether the issue disappears without the SizedBox and notes that a tall-enough suffix icon affecting baseline placement is likely the cause, and that this should not happen for a simple Icon.
summaries.99277: Comments confirm the issue is that the gesture detection region for text selection extends to the label and helper text areas outside the visual text input border. A proposed fix of wrapping the editable text with a GestureDetector was ruled out because _RenderDecoration uses a custom RenderBox. The longer-term fix is applying global selection to TextField, per @chunhtai. An adjustDragOffset utility is shared as a potential building block.
summaries.152729: Confirmed on Windows desktop (stable 3.22.3 and master 3.24.0): the IME candidate window jumps to the correct position only after the second character is typed. Web rendering on the same Windows host shows correct positioning from the first character. Similar behavior observed on macOS.
summaries.95541: Comments narrow the issue to iOS 12 and 13 with the HTML renderer (CanvasKit does not work at all on those versions). No fix is provided; the triage links to the similar #92183. The issue is labeled P4 and held open due to lack of a device to reproduce on.
summaries.120759: Confirmed on Windows 11 touch device (Lenovo Thinkpad Tablet): TextInputType.none does not suppress the on-screen keyboard on Windows touch, affecting CupertinoTextField, TextField, and TextFormField. No fix or PR mentioned.
summaries.157495: Confirmed that SwiftUI TextField handles Hide My Email correctly but Flutter TextField does not: when the user selects Hide My Email, Flutter's TextField loses focus and the generated email address is never populated. The issue persists through stable 3.24 and is open for investigation with no fix or PR.
summaries.97582: Root cause identified: Windows Clipboard History emulates Ctrl+V via a synthetic keyboard event where KeyboardEvent.code is empty and KeyboardEvent.location is 0 (no location); Flutter's keyboard binding asserts that modifier keys must have a valid location, causing the assert failure and freeze in debug mode. Related issue #98460 tracks the empty-code problem. No fix mentioned.
summaries.122490: Comments add detailed documentation of the expected native behavior: iOS always clamps the cursor to the composing region (with one edge case where tapping after the region sometimes cancels composing), and Android/Gboard shows the cursor in the wrong visual position but internally keeps it within the composing region. No fix or PR mentioned.
